title: Werkflow ConnectorDefinition
description: Universal, transport-agnostic envelope for all Werkflow connectors. The `spec.transport.type` discriminator selects the adapter (rest, database, graphql, webhook, mcp, messaging, ...). Each adapter provides its own subschema for `spec.transport.config` and `spec.operations[].transportSpecific`. This envelope is stable across transports — it is what the BPMN designer reads to build operation pickers, schema-driven dropdowns, and validation.

apiVersion.description: Schema version. Bumped on breaking changes to this envelope.

key.description: Stable, unique identifier referenced from BPMN. Lowercase kebab-case. Cannot be changed once published.

displayName.description: Human-readable name shown in the connector picker.

version.description: SemVer. Connector definitions are versioned independently of the platform; multiple versions may coexist for in-flight processes.

category.description: Drives grouping in the connector picker.

icon.description: URL or data URI for the picker icon.

license.description: SPDX expression. Required for community-marketplace connectors.

transport.description: Transport selector. The `type` field is a discriminator; `config` is validated against the adapter-specific schema for that type.

config.description: Transport-specific configuration. Validated by the adapter subschema for the selected `type`. See rest-transport.schema.json, database-transport.schema.json, etc.

auth.description: Supported authentication schemes this connector CAN use — a requirement declaration, like OpenAPI `securitySchemes`. It never carries a secret. The connector definition is tenant-agnostic; the actual credential is bound PER TENANT at registration (TenantApiCredential.authScheme + credentialRef) and stored in OpenBao, then resolved server-side at call time (ADR-020 B.6, ADR-024). A connector may declare multiple profiles; the tenant registration selects one.

operations.description: The set of named operations this connector exposes. Each operation is what a BPMN designer drags onto the canvas.

components.description: Shared schema definitions referenced by operations via $ref. JSON Schema 2020-12 throughout. Aligned with OpenAPI 3.1's components.schemas.

constraints.description: Cross-cutting limits applied by the engine regardless of transport.

maxResponseBytes.description: Hard cap on response body / result set serialized into a process variable. Default 100KB.

AuthProfile.description: A supported auth scheme. Werkflow-native vocabulary (not OpenAPI's): the `type` values map 1:1 to the engine's TenantApiCredential.authScheme and to an OpenBao-backed credential-type. No secret material appears here — the tenant binds a credentialRef at registration.
AuthProfile.$comment: secret-ref, oauth2-client-credentials, and mtls were removed from the type enum 2026-05-25: secret-ref/oauth2 never functioned and were dropped in B.6; mtls is unbuilt (needs a per-tenant client keystore, not header injection) and will be re-added only when implemented. The `secretKey` field was also removed (pre-OpenBao SecretsResolver model).

config.description: Auth-scheme-specific, non-secret config. For api-key: the header name (the value is the OpenBao secret). Reserved for future schemes; never holds secret material.

id.description: Stable identifier referenced from BPMN. camelCase. Unique within the connector.

category.description: Drives icon and grouping in the designer. `list` and `search` operations are paginatable; `subscribe` is for inbound.

input.description: JSON Schema 2020-12 describing the operation's input. Drives the designer's input form. Process variables are mapped against this schema.

output.description: JSON Schema 2020-12 describing the operation's output. Drives the designer's output dropdowns and JSONPath suggestions.

pagination.description: Optional. Required for `list`/`search` category operations. Adapter-specific pagination behavior is in `transportSpecific`.

errors.description: Maps adapter-specific error states (HTTP status, SQL error codes, exceptions) to BPMN-level error semantics (retry, BPMN error code, soft-fail).

examples.description: Sample input/output pairs. Powers the designer's preview and feeds the BPMN test runner.

transportSpecific.description: Operation-level config that is meaningful only to the selected transport. Validated by the adapter's operation subschema. Examples: REST = method/path/body template; DB = named query reference; GraphQL = operation document.

strategy.description: Pagination strategy. The adapter implements the mechanics; this enum selects which mechanic. `keyset` is for DB; `link-header` is for REST; `cursor`/`page-token` are typical SaaS REST; `offset` is the legacy choice (avoid).

maxPages.description: Hard cap on auto-paginated fetch loops. Prevents runaway fetches.

mode.description: How the BPMN designer wants pagination handled. `fetch-all` loops internally and returns the merged list. `page-by-page` exposes each page to a multi-instance subprocess. `first-page-only` is the safe default.

code.description: Werkflow error code, surfaced as BPMN error event code when onError=THROW_BPMN_ERROR. e.g., NOT_FOUND, RATE_LIMITED, AUTH_FAILED, TIMEOUT.

match.description: Adapter-specific match condition. e.g., REST: { httpStatus: 404 }; DB: { sqlState: '23505' }.

input.description: Sample input matching the operation's input schema.

output.description: Sample output matching the operation's output schema.
